title: Hubertia heimii
taxonomy: Hubertia heimii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hubertia, which is a group of plants commonly found in tropical regions. The genus Hubertia is placed in the tribe Eupatorieae, a subfamily of plants known for their distinctive inflorescence and infundibuliform capitula. Within the tribe Eupatorieae, Hubertia heimii is further classified as a member of the subtribe Ageratinae. This classification is based on morphological and molecular characteristics that distinguish Hubertia heimii from other members of the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hubertia heimii, also known as the "Brazilian feather-top plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hubertia heimii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
